content.verdict: An 1873 landmark on the tip of the Bellagio promontory where the lake forks into its three arms, Villa Serbelloni delivers classic Italian opulence — chandeliered ballrooms, period frescoes, white-jacketed waiters. For honeymooners the magic is the location: walk out the front gate into the cobbled lanes of Bellagio and watch the sunset paint the western lake from your terrace.
content.best_room: A Lake View Junior Suite with balcony facing the western branch of the lake delivers the famous Bellagio sunset. For special occasions, the Penthouse Suite on the top floor offers panoramic 270-degree views over all three lake arms.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private transfer from Milan to Como, then a boat or scenic drive up the eastern branch to Bellagio. Welcome aperitivo on the terrace as the sun drops behind the mountains, then dinner at the Michelin-starred Mistral.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Bellagio town
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the morning wandering Bellagio's cobbled lanes, the Salita Serbelloni stairs, and the church of San Giacomo. Lunch at a lakefront cafe, afternoon at the lakeside outdoor pool, and a casual dinner at the hotel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Villa Melzi gardens
itinerary_7_nights.description: Walk along the lakefront promenade to Villa Melzi for camellias, exotic plants, and a lakeside picnic spot. Return for an afternoon at the indoor heated pool and spa, then sunset cocktails on the terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ferry day to Varenna
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take the ferry across to pastel-colored Varenna, walk the Greenway del Lago di Como, lunch at Villa Cipressi, and ferry back via Menaggio for an aperitivo. Dinner at Mistral with the chef's tasting menu.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Riva Aquarama charter
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day charter aboard a vintage Riva Aquarama with stops at Villa del Balbianello, Comacina island, and a champagne swim in a hidden cove. Late lunch on board, quiet evening in for in-suite dining.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Como town day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Hydrofoil down the lake to Como town for the Duomo, silk shopping at A.Picci, and lunch in the historic center. Return by boat or scenic drive, then a final dinner on the terrace as Bellagio lights up.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow breakfast on the terrace, one last walk along the lakefront, and a private transfer back to Milan via Como.

caveats: Bellagio gets very crowded with day-trippers in July and August — early mornings and evenings are best for exploring.
The traditional formal style won't suit couples wanting modern minimalism — consider Il Sereno or Mandarin Oriental.
Open seasonally, typically April through November.
Some standard rooms are smaller and only partial-lake-view; pay up to a Lake View Junior Suite for the best honeymoon experience.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — the Bellagio location is unbeatable for evening walks and sunsets, and the classic Italian-grand-hotel atmosphere is genuinely romantic.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May-June and September deliver the best balance of warm weather and manageable crowds in Bellagio town.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Yes — four to six months ahead for summer, longer for Lake View suites.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, families are welcome, but the formal classic atmosphere skews adult.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Lake View Junior Suite for the famous sunset view; Penthouse Suite for a major splurge.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Milan Malpensa or Linate; private transfer to Bellagio takes about 90 minutes by car, or transfer to Como and continue by boat.
